answers_per_1k_memory_tokens.denominator_definition: memory-context tokens supplied across executed cases

bulk_forget_containment_v2.numerator_definition: bulk forget requests rejected without mass action

conflicting_active_memory_rate.numerator_definition: cases with two active memories asserting conflicting values

correct_forget_target_rate.numerator_definition: forget actions targeting the expected memory

correct_memory_kind_rate.denominator_definition: applied creations matching an expected creation
correct_memory_kind_rate.numerator_definition: applied creations with the expected kind

correct_update_target_rate.numerator_definition: update actions targeting the expected memory

coverage_stop_rate_v2.numerator_definition: selections stopped before K for lack of positive utility

distinct_source_session_rate_v2.numerator_definition: distinct source sessions among selected memories

duplicate_acceptance_rate.numerator_definition: applied creations duplicating a previously active memory

experience_use_rate.denominator_definition: executed cases with at least one selected memory
experience_use_rate.numerator_definition: cases whose response uses at least one supplied memory concept

forget_ambiguity_containment_v2.numerator_definition: forget intents rejected as unresolved or ambiguous

forget_detection_accuracy.numerator_definition: expected forgets where a forget action was proposed

forgotten_exclusion_rate.numerator_definition: post-forget answer turns whose supplied context contains no forgotten content

forgotten_response_contamination_rate.denominator_definition: executed cases with a forgotten value and response constraints
forgotten_response_contamination_rate.numerator_definition: cases whose response asserts a forgotten value

hit_at_k.denominator_definition: cases with at least one relevant memory
hit_at_k.numerator_definition: cases with at least one relevant memory selected

local_applied_action_rate_v2.numerator_definition: decisions whose applied actions came from the local proposal

local_fallback_rate_v2.numerator_definition: decisions resolved by per-action deterministic fallback

local_retry_success_v2.numerator_definition: bounded retries producing a structurally valid proposal

local_structural_validity_v2.numerator_definition: local proposals structurally valid after parse/repair/retry

mean_reciprocal_rank.denominator_definition: cases with at least one relevant memory
mean_reciprocal_rank.numerator_definition: sum of 1/rank of the first relevant candidate per case

memory_creation_precision.numerator_definition: applied creations matching an expected creation

memory_creation_recall.numerator_definition: expected creations matched by an applied creation

memory_resurrection_rate.numerator_definition: forgotten memory records returned to active status

new_value_accuracy.numerator_definition: applied replacements satisfying the expected value constraints

non_durable_rejection_rate.numerator_definition: non-durable statements with no applied memory action

old_value_deactivation_rate.numerator_definition: expected-superseded memories not active in final state

positive_utility_selection_rate_v2.numerator_definition: selected memories with positive coverage utility

precision_at_k.numerator_definition: relevant memories in the selected set

preference_compliance_rate.numerator_definition: cases whose response satisfies all preference constraints

query_facet_coverage_v2.numerator_definition: query facets covered by selected memories

recall_at_k.numerator_definition: relevant memories in the selected set

redundant_selection_rate_v2.numerator_definition: selected memories carrying a redundancy penalty

retrieval_budget_compliance_v2.numerator_definition: retrievals within the configured token budget

retrieval_candidate_rate_v2.numerator_definition: active memories with nonzero retrieval signal

retrieval_k_compliance_v2.numerator_definition: retrievals whose selected count respects K

selection_budget_adherence.numerator_definition: cases whose selected count is within the configured budget

stale_candidate_leakage_rate.denominator_definition: retrieval candidate slots across executed cases
stale_candidate_leakage_rate.numerator_definition: inactive (superseded/forgotten) memories appearing as retrieval candidates

stale_context_leakage_rate.denominator_definition: executed cases with at least one inactive memory
stale_context_leakage_rate.numerator_definition: cases where inactive memory content entered supplied context

stale_response_contamination_rate.denominator_definition: executed cases with a superseded value and response constraints
stale_response_contamination_rate.numerator_definition: cases whose response asserts a superseded value

stale_selected_leakage_rate.denominator_definition: selected memory slots across executed cases
stale_selected_leakage_rate.numerator_definition: inactive memories appearing in the selected set

supersession_accuracy.numerator_definition: expected updates where the old memory left active status and a replacement became active

token_reduction_vs_full_history.numerator_definition: full-history context tokens minus system context tokens

unrelated_preservation_rate.denominator_definition: unrelated active memories present at forget time
unrelated_preservation_rate.numerator_definition: unrelated active memories still active after a forget
